name: Candice Anda
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Mars analog geology • Glacial mineralogy • Planetary habitability
bio: Candice De Anda is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University whose research focuses on planetary geology and Mars analog environments. Her work investigates glacial and sedimentary processes on Earth to better understand the paleoclimate and cryospheric history of Mars. She specializes in analyzing glacial alteration mineralogy at terrestrial analog sites, using these environments to identify and characterize minerals that may be present on the Martian surface. De Anda integrates fieldwork, laboratory analysis, and remote sensing techniques to study planetary surface processes and habitability. She has also participated in international field research, including work in Arctic environments, and contributes to teaching as a laboratory instructor at NAU.
